steps.explanation: Apply the Factor Theorem: if $f(a) = 0$, then $(x - a)$ is a factor of $f(x)$. The three x-intercepts give three zeros: $x = -3$, $x = 1$, and $x = 4$.
Identify which factor appears among the answer choices. The factors are $(x+3)$, $(x-1)$, and $(x-4)$. Only $(x-1)$ matches a listed choice.

Evaluate all choices:
  • Choice A — $x + 1$: This factor would correspond to zero $x=-1$, which is not one of the x-intercepts. ✗
  • Choice B — $x + 4$: This factor would correspond to zero $x=-4$, which is not one of the x-intercepts. ✗
  • Choice C — $x - 1$: This factor corresponds to zero $x=1$, which is one of the x-intercepts, so it must be a factor. ✓
  • Choice D — $x - 3$: This factor would correspond to zero $x=3$, which is not one of the x-intercepts. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
